description: Interactive single-file research tools for the AI-datacenter capex cycle and quant strategy modeling. Educational only — not investment advice.

tools.title: Actionable Market Brief
tools.blurb: One clean, low-noise cockpit: what changed that you should act on, and what's coming. Regime, sector rotation (leading->lagging flips), momentum changes, gamma flip and call/put walls, events with option-implied moves and scenario probabilities, and a watchlist roll-up — each deep-linking the tool that owns the detail. A live layer computed in-browser from the shared rlData cache (no refetch) plus an agent layer refreshed 4x/day. Estimates, not facts — educational only, not investment advice.

tools.title: Market Heatmap Lab
tools.blurb: Whole-market treemap — where the money is, at a glance. Constituents grouped by sector (or the 11 sector ETFs), sized by index weight, dollar-volume or equal, colored by return over 1 day / 1 week / 1 month. A Simple cockpit (one breadth verdict + leaders/laggards; steer the color window / size metric / grouping) with a Power drill-down (per-sector breadth, a sortable constituent table, and sigma-vs-sector outliers). Cache-first from the shared rlData store — reused across tools, never refetched. Educational only — not investment advice.

tools.title: Unusual Options Activity Lab
tools.blurb: Which option strikes lit up today, relative to their own normal — an honest EOD positioning proxy, NOT a real-time trade tape (there is no free live options feed). Scans liquid names' chains for anomalous volume, vol/OI, premium notional and IV, ranks the standouts, and reads the net call-vs-put premium lean. Simple feed + Power table. Chains fetched best-effort (Yahoo + proxies), cached and reused on reload. Never infers buy/sell side. Educational only — not investment advice.

tools.title: Intraday Tape & Volume-Profile Lab
tools.blurb: Intraday technical-analysis lab: read who controls the session (trend-following algos pinned to VWAP vs emotional retail flow) and where support/resistance is. Session VWAP with sigma bands, a session volume profile with a buy/sell delta (an up/down-volume proxy, not real order flow), the opening range vs the prior day's value area, an algo-vs-retail control read, recent-session analogs, and the 0DTE gamma walls reused from the Options Structure Lab via a shared browser cache (no refetch). One Simple verdict plus a Power view; everything computed in-browser; editable watchlist.

tools.title: Swing Structure & Market-Regime Lab
tools.blurb: Swing technical-analysis lab (days-to-weeks): the 20/50/200-day moving-average stack, a composite volume profile with durable HVN/LVN shelves, market-structure and chart-pattern detection with descriptive analog odds, an accumulation/distribution read, an in-browser Fear-and-Greed + VIX regime engine, and the monthly option magnets reused from the Options Structure Lab via a shared browser cache. One positioning verdict plus a Power view; everything computed in-browser; editable watchlist.

tools.title: Options Structure & Momentum Research Lab
tools.blurb: Map option-implied support/resistance: call/put walls, the gamma-flip regime (net GEX) that says whether dealers pin price or let it trend, max pain, the cash volume-profile POC, per-expiry expected-move cones, IV smile/term/skew/VRP, short interest and a bounded squeeze read. Black-Scholes greeks (delta/gamma/vega/theta + vanna/charm) computed in-browser from the fetched Yahoo option chain; the dealer-gamma sign is a documented toggle; day-over-day OI and wall migration accrue in localStorage with no paid history API; every ticker links to Yahoo with a rich tooltip. Multi-provider price layer (Yahoo no-key + Twelve Data / Alpha Vantage / Finnhub keys shared across the Research Lab). Editable optionable-underlyings watchlist.

tools.title: Gamma Trading & Dealer-Flow Playbook Lab
tools.blurb: Turn the dealer-gamma regime into a playbook — the trading sibling of the Options Structure Lab. Read whether dealers are long gamma (pinning) or short gamma (trending), then run the three classic plays: the JEX / gamma-flip waterfall (first negative-gamma close after an extended rally → opening-drive short + afternoon roll), an in-browser Option Volume Imbalance (OVI) model that weights option volume by gamma and ranks it as a percentile against your own rolling snapshots (open interest is a day old; only volume is real-time), and the gamma-vs-time expiration cycle (OPEX pin, 14/30-day windows, Thu/Fri overextension capitulation). Days-in-regime + OVI history accrue in localStorage. One interactive Simple model steered by stance/aggressiveness/horizon levers, plus a full Power dashboard (net-GEX-by-strike, OVI percentile history, OPEX ladder, dealer-sign toggle). Reuses the Options Lab's cached snapshot and the shared Research-Lab keys — nothing is re-fetched.

tools.title: Sector Rotation & Momentum Research Lab
tools.blurb: Two modes — a Simple, steerable one-line rotation call (guided tape → rotate-into with two-clock entry timing → rotate-out → invalidation) and a full Power dashboard. Pull live Yahoo / Twelve Data price history for the 11 GICS sectors, major indexes, custom equal-weight stock groups (Mag 7, semis, memory, software, AI-infra, banks, homebuilders, nuclear/power — plus your own, built at runtime), and cross-assets (BTC, ETH, GLD, TLT…), then read momentum and its underlying forces: a Relative Rotation Graph (RS-Ratio vs RS-Momentum with trajectory tails), a momentum-acceleration ranking that flags Basing/Peaking turns earlier than price, an ETF-first rotation-suggestions panel (names the sector/group ETF to rotate into/out of; individual stocks only on an extreme concentrated-leadership case), an excess-return heatmap, relative-strength lines, on-balance-volume / money-flow, member breadth for the groups, a rotation-intensity gauge, a correlation matrix plus a rolling pair-correlation divergence monitor (software-vs-BTC, GLD-vs-BTC…), and per-entry risk flags. Then drill into a chosen sector: a company-by-sector momentum heatmap (top constituents coloured by 1M/3M/6M/12M excess, a blend, a risk-adjusted (blend÷vol) read or absolute return, with new-high ▲ and RSI overbought/oversold badges and per-sector leader + dispersion) and a sector-ETF selector that ranks each sector's prominent ETFs on ten parameters — size (AUM), liquidity (21-day dollar ADV), cost (expense), coverage (holdings/segment), concentration (top-10 weight), sector-tracking (daily-return correlation to the SPDR), tracking-error (annualised drift vs the SPDR), max drawdown, distribution yield and Sharpe-like risk-adjusted momentum (over a 3M/6M/12M lookback) — into one reweightable Fit score (adjustable weight sliders + presets, plus β and information-ratio reads and a CSV export) so you can pick the best vehicle when you rotate. Editable external universe (incl. a sectorMap catalog of per-sector constituents + candidate ETFs with size/cost/coverage/concentration/yield reference fields); Yahoo ticker links with detail tooltips; everything computed in-browser.

tools.title: Global Rotation Lab
tools.blurb: FX- and session-aware country ETF rotation across South Korea, Singapore, Philippines, Germany, Japan, Taiwan, India, China, the UK, Australia, Canada, Brazil and Mexico. Simple mode gives a steerable country-allocation queue; Power exposes 21/63/126-day relative momentum, 20/50/200 trend, realized risk, FX confirmation, local-close timing and diversification. Cache-first and auto-hydrated.

tools.title: Real Assets Lab
tools.blurb: Distinct transparent models for gold, silver, bitcoin/crypto, energy, broad commodities, copper, agriculture and platinum. Gold uses USD/rate proxies; bitcoin uses risk appetite plus a stronger volatility/drawdown penalty; silver combines monetary- and industrial-metal confirmation. Simple mode is steerable; Power exposes every score, driver and risk input.

tools.title: AI Capex Strategy Lab
tools.blurb: Model US/CAD supplier-beneficiary equities and ETF-proxy strategies across the AI-datacenter supply chain with per-horizon playbooks (1M/3M/6M/1Y), 80 assets across 13 themes, ETF risk dampers, networking/server/buildout/design-IP presets, a 5-objective optimizer, theme-aware correlation, crowding friction, and a lognormal risk model.

tools.title: MSFT July-Print Margin & EPS Model
tools.blurb: Reconcile the late-July Microsoft FY26 Q4 print and FY27E margins against reported Q1-Q3 actuals: an operating-margin bridge, dual Q4 anchors (consensus vs seasonality), depreciation / price-mix / FX levers, a P/E ladder, an OM heatmap, and a verified memory-shortage cost-cycle overlay with phase presets.

tools.title: ETF Momentum Research Lab
tools.blurb: Pull live Yahoo / Twelve Data price history for a momentum-ETF universe (the etfdb High-Momentum list by AUM, plus QQQ and VGT) and compute performance, risk, drawdowns, correlation, CAPM betas, regime-conditional return estimates and Monte-Carlo projections. 27 ETFs from an editable external JSON, live full holdings + sector weights (Alpha Vantage), 11 hand-drawn charts, a sortable leaderboard, an AUM/equal-weighted basket, and add/remove-any-ticker.

tools.title: Strategy Self-Improvement & Walk-Forward Lab
tools.blurb: The viral self-improving trading agent loop, done honestly: set an explicit numeric goal (target CAGR, Sharpe floor, max-drawdown ceiling), run a transparent trend/momentum rule, then let a scientific-method search change ONE variable at a time and keep only the changes that survive out-of-sample walk-forward scoring — with an overfitting / multiple-testing discount so you trust the out-of-sample column, not the in-sample fit. Fully in-browser on deterministic, seed-reproducible synthetic paths (no live data); accepted single-variable improvements accrue in a per-scenario localStorage ledger. Educational only.

tools.title: Strategy Validation & Real-Data Walk-Forward Lab
tools.blurb: The real-data sibling of the Strategy Self-Improvement Lab: validate a mechanical rule on live Yahoo / Twelve Data history the honest way — out-of-sample walk-forward scoring with embargoed folds (no leakage), cross-instrument robustness (held k/N tickers — an edge that works on only one ticker is luck), and a Deflated Sharpe Ratio that discounts for how many variants you tried. Answers what the signal tools can't: is this edge real, or curve-fit? In-browser; a clearly-labelled synthetic-demo path when live data is blocked. Educational only.

tools.title: Smart-Money & Congressional-Flow Lab
tools.blurb: Read disclosed insider (Form 4), congressional (STOCK Act) and institutional (13F) buying, score cluster-consensus conviction, then apply the disclosure-lag haircut the hype videos skip to see how much edge survives by the time you can legally copy the trade. One reality-check verdict plus a Power ledger; everything computed in-browser on editable, illustrative (synthetic) filing data. Educational only, not investment advice.

tools.title: Florida Waterfront × Masters Water-Polo Screener
tools.blurb: Screen Florida WATERFRONT markets (lake / river / intracoastal / canal) to a $1-2M, 2,500-3,000 sqft, land/privacy brief, gated by whether they sit within a ~40-minute drive of a Masters (adult) water-polo practice pinned on a Florida map. Each market is annotated with insurance, flood and storm-surge risk. Drive-time is an approximate straight-line estimate (not a routed isochrone) and the Masters-club layer is a seed to verify. In-browser on an editable waterfront-polo-universe.json. Research screen, not advice.
